trip.title: Pacific Coast Road Trip — Seattle to Los Angeles

travelers.notes: first time on the Pacific coast; all under 21 (US drinking age)

vehicle.notes: under-25 young-driver surcharge; put all 3 on contract to rotate drivers

return.note: NEEDS REBOOKING - original was 2026-08-12 18:59. Verify fare class: Delta Basic Economy generally cannot be changed. Book latest departure possible (8-9pm) to fit Griffith Observatory.

decisions_made: Skipped Olympic Peninsula entirely (too far north, added ~200mi)
Added a 6th day (return Aug 13) which ELIMINATED the overnight-split drive - now sleep in a bed every night
Sleep in Salinas not Monterey (Car Week Aug 7-16 = sold out/$500+)
Sleep Garberville not Eureka (avoids 45min backtrack to Avenue of the Giants)
Sleep Pismo on night 5 (further south = shorter final morning to Malibu)
Skip Hearst Castle tour in favor of free Piedras Blancas elephant seals
User chose: surf lesson + otter kayak + SoCal swimming (declined ATV dunes)
hard_constraints: Monterey Car Week Aug 7-16: do NOT book Monterey/Carmel/Big Sur lodging
Big Sur Hwy 1 open but Rocky Creek Bridge has 24/7 one-way signal control through Aug 31 (<=15min delay) - check Caltrans QuickMap
Fern Canyon requires free day-use reservation (May15-Sep15) + $12 CASH
Car not available until 17:00 on Aug 8 - Day 1 morning is car-less (use Link light rail)
Must be airside at LAX ~3hrs before Aug 13 departure

safety.warning: Sneaker waves on OR/N-CA coast surge 150+ft up beach with no warning; 24+ West Coast deaths since 2012. Cold water causes loss of limb control in seconds. Rules: never turn back on ocean, stay off beach logs, keep well up the sand. Swimming only in SoCal (lifeguarded, ~69F).

must_book_ahead: Rooms: Seaside, Bandon/Coos Bay, Garberville, Salinas, Pismo

stops.name: SEA — Seattle-Tacoma Airport
stops.desc: Land 12:27 PM. ★ BEST FIX: move the Hertz pickup earlier if you can (most reservations change free) — grab it ~1:30 PM and you get a real half-day. Otherwise ride the Link light rail downtown (~$3, ~40 min) and back for 5 PM.

stops.name: Pike Place Market
stops.desc: Link to Westlake, walk down to the market. Chowder, Piroshky Piroshky, the gum wall. Everything below is walkable from here — no car needed.

stops.name: Waterfront + Seattle Great Wheel
stops.desc: Straight downhill from Pike Place on the rebuilt waterfront promenade. 175-ft Ferris wheel out over Elliott Bay, enclosed gondolas, ~12 min, big Sound + skyline views.

stops.name: Seattle Water Taxi (Pier 50)
stops.desc: Cheapest great thing in Seattle: passenger ferry to West Seattle. Best skyline-and-Rainier view in the city, Alki Beach on the far side. ~1 hr round trip.

stops.name: Space Needle + Chihuly Garden
stops.desc: One Monorail hop from downtown. Go up the Needle, or (better value) the Chihuly Garden & Glass next door. MoPOP — music/sci-fi/gaming — is right there too.

stops.name: Sleep: Seaside / Cannon Beach
stops.desc: Car @ 5 PM → depart ~5:30 → dinner in Astoria (Fort George) ~7:30 → Seaside ~9 PM. ~3.5–4 hr, 185 mi. Last stretch is dark, just cruising.

stops.name: Ecola State Park
stops.desc: THE cliff view of Haystack Rock, best in morning light. Reopened Apr 2026 after landslide repairs — check advisories.

stops.name: Cannon Beach — Haystack Rock
stops.desc: 235-ft monolith, puffins, tide pools at low tide. ⚠ Sneaker waves — stay up the beach, off the logs.

stops.name: Hug Point
stops.desc: At low tide, walk around the point to a hidden waterfall on the sand, sea caves, and an old wagon road carved into the cliff.

stops.name: Neahkahnie Mountain Viewpoint
stops.desc: Roadside pullout in Oswald West SP with a near-180° view — one of the highest on the coast. Easy to miss the turnout.

stops.name: Cape Kiwanda
stops.desc: Climb the big sandstone dune; lunch at Pelican Brewing right on the sand.

stops.name: Cape Perpetua — Thor's Well
stops.desc: Highest driveable viewpoint on the OR coast. Thor's Well & Devil's Churn best ~1 hr before high tide (check the chart). Captain Cook Trail to tide pools — safe, great.

stops.name: Heceta Head Lighthouse
stops.desc: Most-photographed lighthouse in Oregon, on a cliff over a cove.

stops.name: Sea Lion Caves
stops.desc: Elevator down to the largest sea cave in the U.S. — ~200 wild sea lions inside. Right on 101 just south of Heceta Head.

stops.name: Sandboarding — Sand Master Park
stops.desc: World's first sandboard park, in the Oregon Dunes. Rent a board and ride 40+ ft dunes. The most fun thing on Day 2 if you're on schedule.

stops.name: Sleep: Bandon / Coos Bay
stops.desc: Sunset at Bandon's Face Rock ~8:25 PM. Cheap motels in Coos Bay; Bandon is prettier.

stops.name: Bandon / Face Rock
stops.desc: Sea stacks and wide beach before the drive south.

stops.name: Samuel Boardman Scenic Corridor
stops.desc: North of Brookings — the best cliff pullouts in Oregon. Natural Bridges & Secret Beach. Lunch in Brookings before crossing into CA.

stops.name: Klamath River Overlook
stops.desc: High bluff over the Klamath river-mouth — the least-crowded great overlook on the coast. Gray whales offshore.

stops.name: Prairie Creek Redwoods — Fern Canyon
stops.desc: World's tallest trees. Fern Canyon's 50-ft fern walls are the showstopper — needs a free day-use reservation + $12 cash. Rough access road. Watch for Roosevelt elk.

stops.name: Avenue of the Giants
stops.desc: 31-mile old-growth scenic alternate to 101. Walk among the giants at Founders Grove.

stops.name: Shrine Drive-Thru Tree
stops.desc: Roadside classic on the Avenue: drive your car THROUGH a living redwood. Cheesy, quick, great photo.

stops.name: Sleep: Garberville
stops.desc: Just south of Avenue of the Giants — cheap motels, no backtracking. THIS is the night the extra day bought you: no more overnight-split drive. Sleep properly, fuel up for Day 4.

stops.name: Golden Gate — Battery Spencer
stops.desc: Marin Headlands overlook for the classic bridge-and-city shot. You now hit this in DAYLIGHT instead of 11 PM — the other big win from the extra day.

stops.name: Elkhorn Slough kayak — sea otters
stops.desc: MAIN EVENT: Moss Landing has the densest sea otter population in California. Flat, calm, protected water — no surf, no cold-shock risk, otters/seals/pelicans within feet of the kayak. Book ahead; leave Garberville by 7:30 AM to make the launch window.

stops.name: Sleep: Salinas (NOT Monterey)
stops.desc: 20 min inland and cheap — Monterey is sold out for Car Week. Rest up; Big Sur is tomorrow.

stops.name: Point Lobos
stops.desc: Cypress Grove / Bird Island loops at the north gateway to Big Sur. Small lot fills fast — get there early. China Cove is stunning.

stops.name: Garrapata State Park — Soberanes Point
stops.desc: Unmarked turnouts (numbered gates 13–19), empty bluff trails. The Big Sur hardly anyone stops for.

stops.name: Bixby Creek Bridge
stops.desc: The most photographed bridge on the coast. Use marked turnouts. Expect the Rocky Creek one-way delay (≤15 min).

stops.name: Pfeiffer Beach
stops.desc: Purple-tinged sand & the keyhole arch. Narrow access road, $12 cash.

stops.name: Nepenthe
stops.desc: Cliffside patio 800 ft above the ocean. Café Kevah below is cheaper.

stops.name: McWay Falls
stops.desc: 80-ft waterfall onto a cove beach. Overlook trail closed through 2026 — view from the roadside.

stops.name: Sand Dollar Beach & Jade Cove
stops.desc: Big Sur's longest beach + the cove where you can find washed-up jade in the rocks at low tide.

stops.name: Ragged Point
stops.desc: Last big pullout at the south end of Big Sur — short trail to a sweeping cliff view, plus café/gas/restrooms.

stops.name: Piedras Blancas Elephant Seals
stops.desc: Hundreds of elephant seals on the beach, free boardwalk. Better value than a Hearst Castle tour.

stops.name: Morro Bay — Morro Rock
stops.desc: Giant volcanic plug rising from the bay. Fish & chips on the Embarcadero. (Backup otter-kayak spot if Elkhorn didn't work out.)

stops.name: Sleep: Pismo Beach
stops.desc: Beach town, cheap motels, and it shortens tomorrow's run to Malibu. Walk the pier at sunset (~7:55 PM).

stops.name: Surf lesson — Malibu (Surfrider)
stops.desc: Leave Pismo ~7 AM (~2.5 hr). Wetsuit included — water's ~69°F here, the warmest of the trip. Surfrider is a long, gentle, forgiving break: the classic first-timer wave. Book ahead.

stops.name: El Matador State Beach
stops.desc: Sea-stack photos, steep stairs to the sand. Quick stop after surfing (it's just west of Surfrider — grab it on the way in if easier).

stops.name: Point Dume
stops.desc: Short climb to a headland with a sweeping Malibu view; sea lions below.

stops.name: Santa Monica — swim + beach
stops.desc: THE swim stop: ~69°F water, lifeguarded, gentle. Pier, Route 66 sign, Venice boardwalk & canals a short walk south. Actually get in the water here.

stops.name: Griffith Observatory / Hollywood Sign
stops.desc: The Hollywood Sign view. Fits ONLY if you rebooked a late (8–9 PM) departure — Griffith is inland, opposite LAX. If your flight is early evening, skip it and keep the beach.

stops.name: LAX — Los Angeles Airport
stops.desc: Return the Hertz car, be airside ~3 hrs before departure. Budget generously for LA traffic. Book the latest departure you can to squeeze in Griffith.
